evidence.correctness_and_freshness: Endpoints (/api/v10/oauth2/token, /applications/{app}/guilds/{guild}/commands) and scopes are internally consistent. However, Discord interaction signature verification uses Ed25519, not HMAC-SHA256 — the verify_discord_signature implementation is incorrect and will reject all real Discord interactions.
evidence.executability: FastAPI handler signature for /interactions uses `body: bytes` and header params without Header(...) dependency — FastAPI will treat them as query params, breaking signature verification. Passing Authorization via query param to /user and /guilds is unusual but parseable. No sandbox-infeasible patterns (no ngrok/sudo). Multiple non-obvious fixes needed.
evidence.completeness: All six pieces present: (a) acquisition steps, (b) pip/npm in bash, (c) env template, (d) backend code, (e) frontend code, (f) test commands. Env vars listed match those referenced. Slight prose around 'YOUR_CLIENT_ID' placeholders but extractable.
evidence.citation_anchoring: Cites docs.discord.com URLs (note: actual canonical is discord.com/developers/docs) inline as [1][5][10]. Mix of official-looking and third-party (github, readthedocs). Domain 'docs.discord.com' may be fabricated — Discord's official docs live at discord.com/developers/docs.

rubric_notes: docs_unavailable=true; scored on internal consistency. Major correctness issue: HMAC-SHA256 used instead of Ed25519 for Discord signature verification — Discord explicitly requires Ed25519. This is a fabricated/incorrect core supporting call, capping correctness at 2. Discord docs URL 'docs.discord.com' is non-canonical (real is discord.com/developers/docs).
